topic: India activist on hunger strike remains stable
headline: SONAM WANGCHUK ENTERS 17TH DAY OF HUNGER STRIKE, HEALTH STABLE DESPITE LOSING 8 KGS
summary: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk has entered the 17th day of a hunger strike, demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over exam paper leaks. Despite his condition worsening and losing over 8 kilograms, his current health status is reported as stable. The hunger strike is in solidarity with the founder of the Cockroach Janta Party, who is also protesting the minister's handling of the leaks. Wangchuk's protest is being held in central New Delhi, with a medical team attending to him around the clock.
